insight: Chrono Filter has synergy with Steelforge: Steelforge makes it easy to spend BB, so if RBB (Chrono + Blastforge) would be hard to spend on off-turns, you can treat RBBB as RB by clicking Steelforge instead, effectively solving Chrono Filter's off-turn resource awkwardness.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion about Chrono Filter's weaknesses and units that help manage its off-turn economy.

insight: Conduit helps fix Chrono Filter's off-turn problem by allowing the player to alternate between GG and RB production each turn, smoothing out the two-color resource pattern Chrono Filter creates.
author: amalloy
context: Discussion about units that pair well with Chrono Filter to manage its alternating-turn economy.

insight: Chrono Filter pairs especially well with Xaetron because Xaetron rewards having two alternating main-turn plans, which aligns naturally with Chrono Filter's odd/even turn structure.
author: apooche
context: Discussion of units that synergize with Chrono Filter's off-turn resource pattern.

insight: Chrono Filter is often considered bad by experienced players unless it helps access a unit with an otherwise awkward tech cost (e.g., Wincer); in most other cases it requires careful planning around the off-turn and is not simply an efficient unit to grab cheaply.
author: amalloy
context: Community debate on whether Chrono Filter is overvalued by newer players or genuinely efficient.

insight: Bloodrager is considered balanced and appropriately punishing: it heavily penalizes strategies that try to wall up and absorb, which is consistent with red's intended aggressive identity. Lancetooth, by contrast, is considered problematic because it punishes players for simply defending.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of a Reddit Prismata wishlist post about unit balance concerns.

insight: When you breach (deal enough damage to eliminate all defending units), damage is assigned on your own turn; but if you do not breach, damage is not dealt until the opponent's turn. This creates an asymmetry: you can snipe a Tia Threnody by breaching and assigning 1 damage to it, but you cannot snipe it if you only have 1 damage going into a blocking Tia.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of when a player loses on their own turn versus the opponent's turn, involving breach mechanics.

insight: A player's units die at the end of their own turn (e.g., Gauss Cannon charges firing), and the opponent defends at the start of their own turn. If all your units die at the end of your turn, your opponent does not need to defend, and you lose on your own turn.
author: masn6811
context: Clarification on turn structure when trying to lose on a specific turn for an achievement involving Pacifist Bot.

insight: The ctrl-click defense assignment is smarter than expected: when ctrl-clicking a unit to prioritize its defense, the game optimally reassigns other defenders. For example, with EEW+Feral against 4 damage, ctrl-clicking the Feral caused the game to lose EE onto W, because once EE was lost there was no longer a reason to absorb onto the Feral.
author: amalloy
context: Amalloy noticed unexpected behavior from ctrl-click defense assignment during a game.

insight: To lose on the opponent's turn (relevant for certain achievements), using Centrifuge and Redeemers is faster than using Plexo Cell and Cluster Bolt, though Plexo + Cluster is easier to execute. The key principle is giving the opponent more damage than the health you give yourself.
author: consensus
context: Community discussion about methods to lose on the opponent's turn for the Pacifist Bot achievement.

insight: Player 2 is still favored at the top of the Prismata ladder. A proposed fix is giving P1 a special starting unit (buildtime ~5, lifespan 1-2) that can be sacrificed to gain GG — functioning as a one-use anti-trinity click — which would most benefit P1 in exactly the sets where P2 was already advantaged.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of P1 vs P2 balance at high-level play and a concrete mechanic proposal to address it.
